Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Coding My Handwriting 50 upvotes, 3 comments
  2. An investigation of the forces behind the age-verification bills 92 upvotes, 10 comments
  3. Okmain: How to pick an OK main colour of an image 241 upvotes, 46 comments
  4. Can I run AI locally? 975 upvotes, 258 comments
  5. 1M context is now generally available for Opus 4.6 and Sonnet 4.6 322 upvotes, 93 comments
  6. Using Thunderbird for RSS 75 upvotes, 16 comments
  7. OpenTelemetry for Rust Developers 38 upvotes, 6 comments
  8. The Wyden Siren Goes Off Again: We'll Be "Stunned" by NSA Under Section 702 387 upvotes, 117 comments
  9. I Found 39 Algolia Admin Keys Exposed Across Open Source Documentation Sites 89 upvotes, 22 comments
  10. Show HN: Channel Surfer – Watch YouTube like it’s cable TV 426 upvotes, 139 comments
  11. Lost Doctor Who Episodes Found 235 upvotes, 73 comments
  12. Mouser: An open source alternative to Logi-Plus mouse software 215 upvotes, 66 comments
  13. Our Experience with I-Ready 19 upvotes, 2 comments
  14. Hammerspoon 226 upvotes, 81 comments
  15. Games with loot boxes to get minimum 16 age rating across Europe 39 upvotes, 9 comments
  16. Kovan: From Production MVCC Systems to Wait-Free Memory Reclamation 8 upvotes, 0 comments
  17. TUI Studio – visual terminal UI design tool 566 upvotes, 274 comments
  18. Shipping Grayscale Photos at Small Scale 6 upvotes, 0 comments
  19. New 'negative light' technology hides data transfers in plain sight 67 upvotes, 43 comments
  20. Hyperlinks in terminal emulators 86 upvotes, 58 comments
  21. Launch HN: Captain (YC W26) – Automated RAG for Files 45 upvotes, 28 comments
  22. Stanford researchers report first recording of a blue whale's heart rate (2019) 57 upvotes, 38 comments
  23. Qatar helium shutdown puts chip supply chain on a two-week clock 453 upvotes, 413 comments
  24. Launch HN: Spine Swarm (YC S23) – AI agents that collaborate on a visual canvas 87 upvotes, 66 comments
  25. Show HN: Context Gateway – Compress agent context before it hits the LLM 62 upvotes, 45 comments
  26. Your phone is an entire computer 253 upvotes, 247 comments
  27. Source code of Swedish e-government services has been leaked 204 upvotes, 200 comments
  28. John Carmack about open source and anti-AI activists 244 upvotes, 343 comments
  29. Parallels confirms MacBook Neo can run Windows in a virtual machine 206 upvotes, 286 comments
  30. Elon Musk pushes out more xAI founders as AI coding effort falters 344 upvotes, 518 comments

Last generated 11:00am, 14 March 2026. Source here.
